summary_one_line: DTD with a non-empty subset body (at least one of entities/elements/attributes/notations/pentities populated), forcing the ' [' content path.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An XML input whose <!DOCTYPE ...> internal subset declares at least one DTD member (element decl, attribute decl, entity, notation, or parameter entity), so dtd->elements (etc.) is non-NULL and the line-686 conjunction is false, taking the ' [\n' body-dump branch rather than the bare '>' close.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx winning seeds carry DOCTYPE-with-content structure: seed 2/3 contain '<!DOCTYPE a S[<!ELEMENT' and the W role uniquely executes xmlBufDumpElementDecl (W=10, L=0), i.e. an element declaration was parsed into dtd->elements; on the blocker the winner takes the false (body) side (L686 W: T=0 F=10) and proceeds into the children loop (L706 W: T=12 F=10).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive loser seeds reach the DTD node but stay on the empty-DTD true side (L686 L: T=10 F=0) and never enter the body; the loser never invokes xmlBufDumpElementDecl (L=0) and instead exercises the SYSTEM-id path / escaping functions, meaning its parsed DTD has all five member tables NULL.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers share the identical havoc+token mutator stack with no I2S and no CMP_MAP; the only difference is naive_ctx's CtxHook, which folds the caller chain into the edge-map index so that reaching the line-686 edge via the XML_DTD_NODE dispatch in xmlNodeDumpOutputInternal is recorded as a NEW (call-context, edge) bucket. This context-sensitive coverage rewards the intermediate states along the DTD-population path, giving the corpus the structural stepping-stones to build a non-empty-subset DOCTYPE, whereas bare-edge naive collapses those distinct contexts onto already-covered edges and gets no new-coverage signal to retain them.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false side of a five-way AND testing all DTD member tables for NULL; taking it requires at least one table non-NULL (a non-empty DTD subset).
The winner enters the DTD body-dump path (' [') and the children loop, which only happens on the false side.
The winner uniquely executes element-declaration dumping, evidence that dtd->elements was populated.
Winner seeds contain DOCTYPE structure with an element declaration in the internal subset.
On the blocker branch the loser always takes the true (empty-DTD) side and never the false body side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 686  T=0 F=10  T=10 F=0  if ((dtd->entities == NULL) && (dtd->elements == NULL) &&  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Both involved fuzzers use the same havoc/token mutators with no I2S or CMP_MAP; only the context-indexing differs.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
naive's havoc + token stack. No `I2SRandReplace`, no
CMP_MAP.

evidence_trail.claim: The naive_ctx CtxHook folds the call context into the edge-map index so the same edge is a new bucket under a different call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is naive_ctx beating naive on the ctx_coverage technique delta.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is context-sensitive coverage (naive_ctx CtxHook), not I2SRandReplace, so lineage dash-row verification is not applicable. The MECHANISM CONTEXT section confirms naive_ctx has no I2SRandReplace and no CMP_MAP and emits only havoc/token mutation_op names with no ParentInfo-only/dash rows, so an I2S-floor lineage check is not the relevant signal here.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the DTD-body path edge is reached through a single call context (no caller-chain ambiguity) shows naive and naive_ctx resolving the false branch at equal rates, indicating context-indexing gave no stepping-stone advantage.

weakest_evidence_point: The causal link from context-indexed new-coverage buckets to actually retaining the intermediate DTD-population seeds is inferred from mechanism description plus the W-only xmlBufDumpElementDecl hit, not directly observed in lineage.
